title: brassica oleracea subvar. insularis
intro: Brassica oleracea subvar. insularis, also known as wild cabbage or sea kale, is a plant species that belongs to the Brassica family, characterized by its edible leaves, stems, and flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The health benefits of wild cabbage include its ability to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and protect against oxidative stress. Wild cabbage can be prepared in various ways, including as a herbal tea, infused oil, or added to soups and salads, and it is also used as a food ingredient in some traditional dishes.
